topic: A student receives a much lower grade than expected
raw_output:
[story 1]

The red 'D' stared back at me from the graded essay. It felt heavy, a physical weight settling onto my chest. I traced the ink with a fingertip, the texture rough against my skin. My shoulders slumped further, a slow, heavy surrender to the reality of the poor performance. I didn't bother to look up when Professor Albright called my name; I just waited for the inevitable, quiet acknowledgment of failure. Inside, there was a dull, pervasive numbness, as if the color had been leached out of the room. I simply nodded, a small, barely perceptible movement, and gathered my things with an almost robotic slowness. The noise of the bustling classroom seemed distant, muffled by a thick layer of apathy that had coated everything.

[story 2]

My stomach tightened into a cold knot the moment I saw the mark. It was significantly worse than I had anticipated, a stark, undeniable indictment of my effort. I picked up the paper, my hand trembling only slightly, and folded it neatly, as if preparing something for storage rather than an assessment of my own inadequacy. A strange calm washed over me, a sort of quiet resignation. Why fight it? The energy to argue, to even feel the sting of disappointment, just wasn't there. I stared out the window at the rain streaking the glass, observing the world as if it were a distant, irrelevant spectacle. My breath came in shallow, even rhythms, matching the flat line of my mood.

[story 3]

When the transcript arrived, the numbers swam before my eyes. The grade was a significant drop, a noticeable dip in the otherwise steady upward trend I had been aiming for. I sat at my desk, the fluorescent lights humming a monotonous tune overhead. I didn't immediately react. Instead, I picked up my pen, turning it over and over in my fingers, the plastic cool against my palm. There was no surge of anger, no burst of frantic self-recrimination—just a profound stillness. It was as if the disappointment had seeped into my bones, replacing any sharp feeling with a soft, heavy blanket. I let my gaze drift aimlessly across the room, conserving all available internal resources.
